hero.headline: Host a FIFA World Cup 2026™ Public Viewing in Malaysia
hero.subhead: All public screenings of the Tournament in Malaysia require prior written authorization from Total Sports Asia. Register below to begin the process.
hero.ctaPrimary: Apply for a Public Viewing Authorisation

compliance.heading: Public viewings require written permission
compliance.body: Under FIFA regulations, any public exhibition of FIFA World Cup 2026 matches in Malaysia — including in malls, fan zones, public squares, stadium overflow, corporate events, and ticketed venues — requires prior written authorization from Total Sports Asia as the licensed commercial rights operator. Unauthorized public screenings may result in enforcement action under Malaysian law and FIFA's commercial rights framework.

venues.title: Venues & Public Spaces
venues.desc: Malls, fan zones, public squares, stadium overflow

howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.feesNote: Authorisation is granted based on compliance with terms and conditions set by Total Sports Asia Sdn Bhd based on FIFA requirements.

steps.title: Submit Registration Online
steps.desc: Complete the online form with your organisation and venue details.

steps.title: TSA Reviews Application
steps.desc: Our team will review your venue, scope, and intended audience.

steps.title: Receive Written Authorisation
steps.desc: Receive your official written authorisation and commercial terms.

steps.title: Host Your Viewing
steps.desc: Compliant, supported, and eligible for TSA promotional channels.

step1.headline: Let's get your public viewing approved
step1.subhead: About 3 minutes. We'll come back to you within 3 working days.

step4.heading: Your Venue
step4.screenSetup: Number of screens and setup type (optional)

step6.heading: Commercial Details
step6.chargesEntry: Will you charge entry or sell tickets?
step6.sellsFnb: Will F&B be sold during viewings?
step6.sponsorshipInterest: Interested in sponsorship or co-branded activation?

step8.heading: Consent & Submit
step8.consentPdpa: I consent to Total Sports Asia Sdn Bhd collecting and processing the personal data I have provided in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2010 (Malaysia) and TSA's Privacy Notice, for the purpose of processing my public viewing application and related communications.
step8.consentCompliance: I understand that hosting a public viewing of FIFA World Cup 2026 matches in Malaysia without written authorization from Total Sports Asia is a breach of FIFA regulations and may result in enforcement action.

step9.headline: Registration received
step9.body: Thank you. A member of the Total Sports Asia team will be in touch within 3 working days. For urgent enquiries, message us on WhatsApp.

items.q: Do F&B outlets, restaurants, and cafés need authorisation?
items.a: No. F&B outlets, restaurants, cafés, and bars are not required to obtain authorisation from Total Sports Asia to screen FIFA World Cup 2026 matches. Authorisation is required for venues and public spaces hosting dedicated public viewings, and for corporate events.

items.q: What counts as a public viewing that requires authorisation?
items.a: A public viewing is any organised screening of FIFA World Cup 2026 matches where the event is promoted or ticketed, where a specific venue or space is set up primarily for watching the matches, or where a commercial activation is attached. This includes malls, fan zones, public squares, stadium overflow zones, outdoor screens, and corporate or community events.

items.q: How long does the process take?
items.a: Our team reviews all applications within 3 working days of submission. Once approved, you will receive your written authorisation and commercial terms by email.

items.q: Can I screen matches before I receive written authorisation?
items.a: No. Screening matches without prior written authorisation from Total Sports Asia is a breach of FIFA's commercial rights framework and may result in enforcement action. Please ensure your authorisation is in place before your event.

items.q: Do community organisations and NGOs need authorisation?
items.a: Yes. Any organisation — including community groups, NGOs, associations, and government bodies — hosting a dedicated public viewing event requires written authorisation, regardless of whether the event is free or ticketed.

items.q: I have already submitted. What happens next?
items.a: You will receive a confirmation email with your reference ID. A member of the Total Sports Asia team will contact you within 3 working days to discuss your application and next steps.

whatsapp.heading: Prefer to Talk It Through?
whatsapp.body: Message the Total Sports Asia public viewing team directly on WhatsApp.
whatsapp.prefilledMessage: Hi, I'd like to find out more about a FIFA World Cup 2026 public viewing authorisation.

footer.disclaimer: FIFA World Cup 2026™ is a trademark of FIFA. Total Sports Asia is the licensed commercial rights operator for the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Malaysia.
